arts.ttl: Training Report: Alphonso Davies works out individually at Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Alphonso Davies back on the pitch today for the first time since his injury pic.twitter.com/tfpIRWVANH Alphonso Davies has resumed individual training for the first time since his hamstring injury and even completed light exercises with the ball [ @BILD ] pic.twitter.com/kjZI1N8Dw2

arts.ttl: Christoph Freund: “We hold high hopes for Gabriel Vidović at Bayern Munich”
arts.sum: Unfortunately, he didn’t get the playing time we’d hoped for in the first half of the season at Mainz, so we’ve agreed together with the player to terminate the loan early,” Bayern Munich sporting director Christoph Freund said via Bayern’s official website . Unfortunately, nothing worked for Vidović at Mainz as he was left out of the matchday squad five times, thrown to the wolves against Bayern in the DFB Pokal.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Kingsley Coman names Mo Salah, Vinícius, Bukayo Saka as football’s top wingers
arts.sum: Bayern Munich winger Kingsley Coman has seen, done, and won it all at club level in his many years in Bavaria. “[ Liverpool forward] Mo Salah I’d say, because he’s so efficient,” Coman said in his chat on Rio Ferdinand’s podcast earlier in January (as captured by @iMiaSanMia ).

arts.ttl: Tottenham Hotspur manager explains Bayern Munich loanee Mathys Tel’s U-turn, how it demonstrates the right mentality
arts.sum: With the 19-year-old making the decision to leave Bayern Munich just days before the window closing, long standing interest from Arsenal FC, Manchester United and Tottenham Hotspur was re-ignited and led to a dramatic race for the French top talent. Tottenham manager Ange Postecoglou (try saying that name three times fast), who held a conversation with the youngster that was said to have changed his mind, gave his opinion on the matter:

arts.ttl: Man City draw Real & Celtic face Bayern in Champions League play-offs
arts.sum: Manchester City will play Real Madrid while Celtic will face Bayern Munich in the Champions League play-off round.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s very, very late loan of wunderkind Noël Aséko Nkili involved a contract renewal and buy option for Hannover 96
arts.sum: Bayern’s official website confirmed Nkili was granted another contract renewal, this time until 2028, and was loaned out for the next 18 months. Hannover’s official website confirmed there was an option to buy, as well.

arts.ttl: Mathys Tel will be a Tottenham player next season – Ange Postecoglou
arts.sum: Mathys Tel is set to make his Tottenham debut in Thursday’s Carabao Cup semi-final second leg at Liverpool.

arts.ttl: Preview: Bayern Munich vs. Werder Bremen - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Friday's Bundesliga clash between Bayern Munich and Werder Bremen, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou says forward Mathys Tel never rejected Tottenham move
arts.sum: Tel’s loan deal does include the option for Spurs to make the transfer permanent in the summer.

arts.ttl: Man Utd's No.9 answer? Harry Kane's release clause revealed
arts.sum: Harry Kane's contract at Bayern Munich surprisingly contains a release clause, according to German media. Intriguingly, BILD now reports that Musiala will not be the first Bayern player in recent memory - or potentially ever - with a release clause because record signing Harry Kane has had one since he joined the club for €100 million in 2023.
